nodes.title: Plant Pathology
nodes.description: Introduction to the causes of agricultural and forest plant diseases including examination of the relationship of the plant, pathogen, and environment in disease development and disease management. Prerequisites: PBIO 1040, or BIOL 1400 and BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1400 and BCOR 1450, or BCOR 1425, or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: PSS 2170.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Ecological Invasions
nodes.description: Focuses on reading, writing and discussing the primary scientific literature in the field of invasion biology. Invasion biology draws from many different scientific disciplines, including genetics, evolution, population, community and ecosystem ecology. Students will have the opportunity to pursue one of these areas in depth through the preparation of a research paper. Credit not awarded for both PBIO 3220 and PBIO 5220. Prerequisite: BCOR 2100 or NR 2030.
nodes.other_requirements: an equivalent course in Ecology. Students are expected to read the assigned articles and come to every class with questions and comments on the assigned readings. Students should expect to attend every class and complete assignments on the due dates.

nodes.title: Plant Physiology
nodes.description: Study of the plant as a whole, growth and development, water and mineral relations, environmental factors, and regulatory processes. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400 and BCOR 1450, or BIOL 1400 and BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1425; CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1450, or CHEM 1100 and CHEM 1150, or CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1150; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Must register for a PBIO 2040 lab A01 - A02 - A03. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis.  Credit as arranged.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Molecular Plant Cell Biology
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Outline: General Chem w/lab
nodes.description: One-semester survey of principles and concepts of general chemistry, topics covered include bonding, mole ratios, equilibrium, and nuclear chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1100 and CHEM 1102, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1100 and CHEM 1102, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405. $60 fee for lab supplies; Colocated with CHEM 1102A; Must register for CHEM 1100 lab L01-L07; Non-CAS, non - BSAD students only; Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Department permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Department permission. Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Ecuador: Natural History
nodes.description: Provides a hands-on exploration of the unique biodiversity found in the tropical Andes and the Galapagos Islands, while studying ideas of how this great diversity came to be, and examining conservation efforts employed to protect it. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400 and BCOR 1450 or BIOL 1400 and BIOL 1450; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. For intermediate undergraduate students and PACE students. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Master's Project Research
nodes.description: Final project under the direction of a graduate faculty mentor.  Credit as arranged.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission Required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Writing: Science&Investigation
nodes.description: Writing seminar on interviews, opinion writing, feature stories, and effective scientific storytelling. Students conduct interviews, investigate scientific stories of general interest, and write public-facing articles. Prerequisites: PBIO 6330; Field Naturalist Program Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Field Naturalist Program Graduate student. Field Naturalist Grad Students only; Instructor will email class about meeting time and location. Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Plant Systematics
nodes.description: Collection and identification of ferns and flowering plants; survey of prominent Vermont plant families; plant nomenclature, classification, and phylogeny; species concepts and speciation; floral function. Prerequisites: PBIO 1040 or BIOL 1450 or BCOR 1450 or BCOR 1425 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Graduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Department permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Department permission. Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Plants, Food, and Culture
nodes.description: Evaluation of the impact of plants on the aesthetic, cultural, social, medical, and religious lives of peoples of the world. Plant Biology and Biological Science majors will not receive credit for PBIO 1060 as part of program distribution requirements.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students. Students are expected to attend lecture and participate in the class to gain credit. This course places great emphasis on in-class discussions and sharing of ideas, and engagement throughout class periods is expected.

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 2
nodes.description: Principles of organismal biology; nature of scientific inquiry, plant form and function, pollination ecology, animal phylogeny illustrated by comparative anatomy and physiology; animal behavior. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1450 and BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: ASCI, BIOL, BISC, MGEN, MICR, NEUR, PBIO, ZOOL majors should register for BCOR 1450; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre med students register for BIOL 1450

nodes.title: Putting Down Roots in PlantBio
nodes.description: Designed to introduce new Plant Biology majors to the department and the discipline through a variety of in person experiences and hands-on activities featuring the range of plant biology resources and research at UVM. Helps students build connections to the plant biology community, demystify the college experience, and navigate the major. Prerequisite: Plant Biology major.
nodes.other_requirements: Plant Biology major. PBIO majors only

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 1
nodes.description: Principles of cellular biochemistry; cell biology; genetics and evolution. Topics: biochemistry; metabolism, cell structure/function; respiration; photosynthesis; molecular, Mendelian and population genetics; genetics of evolution. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425. Must register for a BIOL 1400 lab; Lab supply fee: $30; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med & PACE students register for BIOL 1400 ZRA Colocated with BIOL 1400 ZRA

nodes.title: General Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Second semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include solutions, kinetics, equilibrium, acid-base chemistry, aqueous ionic equilibria, thermodynamics, electrochemistry, and nuclear chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1450 and CHEM 1455. Prerequisite: CHEM 1400 or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: $60 fee for lab supplies; Degree students only;

nodes.title: Ecology, Ecosystems & Environ
nodes.description: Major ecological concepts and their application. Analysis of form, structure, and function of organisms, populations, communities, ecosystems, and landscapes. Prerequisites: RSENR students or Agroecology or Landscape Design or Environmental Studies majors; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing

nodes.title: Plant Biology Capstone
nodes.description: Culmination of the Plant Biology major; draws together the disciplines of plant biology presented in previous coursework and other student experiences into a single over-arching perception of plant biology and its role in the world; prepares Plant Biology majors for post-graduate success. Prerequisites: Plant Biology major; Senior standing or Junior standing if graduating in December.
nodes.other_requirements: Plant Biology major; Senior standing or Junior standing if graduating in December. Restricted to Seniors in the Plant Biology major; Instructor will email students with meeting location.

nodes.title: Field Naturalist Fundamentals
nodes.description: From bedrock geology to experimental design, conservation science to public communications, prepares students for the varied challenges that field naturalists encounter. Consists of field exercises in natural areas and farmscapes around northwestern Vermont and discussions featuring a diversity of pieces written by naturalists, field scientists, and environmental thinkers. Prerequisite: Field Naturalist Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Field Naturalist Graduate student or Instructor permission. Instructor permission required.

nodes.title: Intro to Ethnobotany
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Field Botany for NR Profession
nodes.description: Identification of flowering plants and ferns; survey of prominent Vermont plant families; natural communities, ecological determinants of plant distribution, especially soils; preparation of herbarium specimens. Prerequisite: Instructor Permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission

nodes.title: Intro to Professional Writing
nodes.description: Writing seminar that focuses on close observation of the natural world and interpreting scientific findings for public audiences. Includes preparation of an animal encounter and science brief, as well as reading of published natural history and ecology essays. Prerequisite: Field Naturalist Program Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Field Naturalist Program Graduate student. Field Naturalist Graduate students only; Contact instructor for meeting location and time

nodes.title: Ecuador: Natural History
nodes.description: Provides a hands-on exploration of the unique biodiversity found in the tropical Andes and the Galapagos Islands, while studying ideas of how this great diversity came to be, and examining conservation efforts employed to protect it. Prerequisites: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Spring break travel course; Ecuador: Natural History in the Galápagos; OIE GoAbroad App Deadline: 11/18/2025; In-country dates 3/7/2026 - 3/14/2026; Location: Ecuador; Instructor permission required; Program fee $3,516 minus $450 deposit; Airfare is additional; Tuition not included in program fee; For entry-level undergraduate students and PACE students. Colocated with PBIO 2890 for Intermediate students. Pre/post work required to cover the entire semester. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: SL:NatureBasedClimateSolutions
nodes.description: Explores the scale of global change while focusing on ways plants can be used to mitigate and adapt to the future climate while enhancing native plant biodiversity. Students will gain practical skills by engaging with community partners and contributing to nature-based climate solutions projects in the local Burlington community. Prerequisites: BIOL 1450, BCOR 1450, or PBIO 1040.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor will email class w/meeting location. Open to Degree and PACE Students. Note that registered students are expected to dedicate one non-class weekend day to a service-learning project in late April.

nodes.title: Cultural Anthropology
nodes.description: Introduction to cultural anthropology, using fieldwork-based concepts and methods to study diverse cultural views and practices, varied forms of social organization, and contemporary global issues.

nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research for the Doctoral Dissertation. Credit as arranged.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Modeling of Ecological Systems
nodes.description: Covers methods used to model ecological and environmental systems with a focus on system dynamics and agent-based modeling. Explores the complexity of ecological systems, including emergent properties and feedbacks between system components. Utilizes the system dynamics software Stella and the agent-based programming language Netlogo. Credit not awarded for both PBIO 3940 and PBIO 5940. Prerequisite: BCOR 2100 or NR 2030 or an equivalent intermediate-level course in ecology and/or evolution.
nodes.other_requirements: an equivalent intermediate-level course in ecology and/or evolution. online - asynchronous. Credit not awarded for both PBIO 3940 and PBIO 5940. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Writing for a Popular Audience
nodes.description: Writing seminar in which students pitch, write, and publish a feature story, essay, or opinion piece in a general publication such as a newspaper, a trade or professional magazine, or website. Also explores news releases, fundraising appeals, and other institutional writing. Prerequisites: PBIO 6330; Field Naturalist Program Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Field Naturalist Program Graduate student. Field Naturalist graduate students (or instructor permission) only.

nodes.title: Outline: Organic & BIOC w/lab
nodes.description: Broad overview of most important facts and principles of organic and biochemistry and interrelationships between these branches of chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1150 and CHEM 1152, CHEM 1580, or CHEM 1582. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100 or CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for a CHEM 1150 lab, L01 - L06; $85 fee: Chem Lab Supplies; Open to Degree and PACE students; Cross listed with CHEM 1152A; total enrollment cap = 142

nodes.title: Botany Seminar
nodes.description: Presentations of personal research by faculty, Graduate students, and outside guest speakers. Attendance required of Plant Biology Graduate students and Seniors in botanical research programs. Without credit.

nodes.title: Landscape Inventory&Assessment
nodes.description: A comprehensive, practical, hands-on training exercise that teaches how professionals go about making sense of a landscape and its many possible values. Designed for Graduate students, the class functions as a pro bono consulting team on a specific land parcel, helping the community sponsor understand the parcel's biota, soils, hydrology, geology, and natural communities. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Instructor Permission Required. Open to Graduate Students. This project requires the use of GIS and GPS.

nodes.title: Accelerated Biology
nodes.description: Selected topics from the full year of introductory biology, compressed into one semester. For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. Pre/co-requisite: CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. By invitation only; for FTFY students with AP score of 4. Instructor permission required. Must register for a BCOR 1425 lab. Must be enrolled in CHEM 1400 if have no CHEM credit.

nodes.title: Int:EthnobotanyInternship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Restricted to Plant Biology Majors; Instructor permission required; PACE Student by Permission and Override

nodes.title: Biology of Fungi
nodes.description: Collect, identify and study major fungal groups, especially basidiomycetes (mushrooms, rusts and smuts), ascomycetes (cup fungi, yeasts and mildews), and affiliated taxa. Extensive field and lab work, with thematic lectures. Prerequisite: PBIO 1040 or BIOL 1450 or BCOR 1450 or BCOR 1425 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Field Naturalist Practicum
nodes.description: Landscape analysis; planning and designing field projects; integrated problem solving. Prerequisite: Field Naturalist student.
nodes.other_requirements: Field Naturalist student

nodes.title: General Chemistry 1
nodes.description: First semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include matter, stoichiometry, gas laws, thermochemistry, quantum theory, atomic structure, electronic configurations, bonding, and intermolecular forces. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1100, CHEM 1102, or CHEM 1405.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 2
nodes.description: An evolutionary perspective to exploring biology. Topics include: patterns of inheritance, Darwinian evolution, evolution of biodiversity, ecology of organisms, human effects on biological systems. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1450 and BIOL 1450.

nodes.title: Ecology and Evolution
nodes.description: Ecosystem and community structure, population growth, species interactions and niche dynamics, population and chromosomal genetics, speciation in fossil records, ecology of animal behavior, applied ecology. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 1
nodes.description: Exploring biology from cells to organisms. Topics include origins of life, ancestral organisms, uni- and multi- cellular energetics, evolution of respiration and metabolism, and the genetic code. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1400 and BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: Intro to Botany
nodes.description: Introduction to plant biology from cells to ecosystems. Explore plant structure and function, evolution, diversity, ecology, and the role of plants in maintaining ecosystem functions and services. Integrates lectures, hands-on labs, and discussion-based activities to build scientific skills, including data analysis, hypothesis testing, and scientific communication. Provides foundational knowledge for advanced study in the plant and life sciences.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for PBIO 1040 lab A01 - A05

nodes.title: Crafting Research Proposals
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
